news.Title: 1 Analyst Puts the Odds of a Tesla and SpaceX Merger at 80%. Here's What That Would Mean for Tesla Investors.
news.Content: For more than two decades, putting a number on a Tesla-SpaceX merger was guesswork because only one of the two companies traded publicly. That changed on June 12, when SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) completed the largest initial public offering (IPO) in history at a valuation near $1.8 trillion. With a public price finally attached to the rocket company, long-running speculation that Elon Musk will fold his two trillion-dollar businesses into one resurfaced. The figures involved are enormous. Electric-car maker Tesla (NASDAQ: ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Speculation about a potential merger between Tesla (TSLA) and SpaceX has resurfaced following SpaceX's $1.8 trillion IPO.
• Wedbush analyst Dan Ives estimates an 80% probability of a merger within a year, citing significant overlap in AI and robotics.
• A combined entity would be valued at over $3 trillion, spanning EVs, robotics, rockets, satellite internet, and AI.
• However, SpaceX leadership expresses a more measured view, and betting markets place near-term merger odds between 25%-40%.
• Potential challenges include setting merger terms due to differing ownership structures and Tesla's high P/E ratio.
news.Keywords: Merger Speculation, IPO, AI Ecosystem, Valuation, Regulatory Considerations

news.Title: 台股天天劇烈波動！他笑問「會不會被關禁閉」　網調侃：要戴電子腳鐐
news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]實習記者陳又綺／綜合報導

近期台股走勢震盪劇烈，大盤頻繁出現數百點甚至上千點的波動，引發投資人熱議。一名網友日前在PTT發文，以半開玩笑的方式提問，台股個股設有 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 近期台股指數波動劇烈，引發投資人對於大盤是否可能被列為「注意股」或受到類似個股的交易限制的討論。
• 網友以幽默方式回應，將週末休市比喻為「關禁閉」，並開玩笑稱大盤可能需要「戴電子腳鐐」。
• 部分討論聚焦於台股現行制度，如漲跌幅限制、T+2交割及警示股制度，與國際市場的差異。
• 有觀點認為，大盤指數的波動幅度與個股標準不同，被列為警示或處置對象的可能性極低。
• 整體而言，近期市場波動被部分投資人認為仍在正常範圍內，與歷次重大股災相比尚屬溫和。
news.Keywords: 台股, 波動, 交易制度, 警示股, 監管措施

news.Title: T-Mobile warns customers that a key service will double in price
news.Content: T-Mobile has been rolling out price increases over the past few months and will soon make another despite elevated customer losses. In January, the carrier raised its Regulatory Programs & Telco Recovery fee, which customers pay on their monthly bills. It also started charging $3 a month for its Apple TV “On Us” perk, which had been free for customers on Plus-level phone plans since 2021. By March, T-Mobile sparked backlash when it began issuing a $35 Device Connection Charge to customers who purchased devices directly ...
news.AI_Summary:
• T-Mobile is implementing price increases on several services, including international calling rates which will double from $0.25 to $0.50 per minute starting June 25.
• The company has also increased charges for its Apple TV 'On Us' perk and introduced a Device Connection Charge for devices purchased directly from Apple.
• These price hikes come amid an increase in T-Mobile's postpaid phone churn rate, which ticked up by 3 basis points year-over-year.
• Customers can avoid higher international call rates by using texting or purchasing International Passes.
• The competitive landscape in the U.S. wireless market is intensifying with MVNOs and satellite services like SpaceX's Starlink Mobile.
news.Keywords: Pricing Strategy, Customer Churn, International Roaming, Competitive Landscape, Wireless Market

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: 台股強勢股帶頭衝！莎莉解析009816靠「這兩招」跟上漲勢.放大複利效果​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
news.Content:
商傳媒 ｜記者陳宜靖／台北報導

不配息的市值型ETF，成長速度是否會更快？這是不少投資人的疑問。近日，理財創作者「想 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 理財創作者「想退休的莎莉」分析不配息市值型ETF 009816，指出其成立至今漲幅約49%，表現亮眼。
• 009816的選股邏輯包含追蹤台灣前五十大企業，成分股近四季需獲利，並加入動能策略分配權重。
• 該ETF的策略有助於跟上台股強勢股的漲勢，提升整體報酬表現，適合追求成長的投資人。
• 不配息設計有助於資本累積，但投資人無法取得現金流入，需留意流動性規劃。
• 動能策略在市場風格快速輪動時可能面臨滯後調整的挑戰，投資前應審視自身財務目標與風險承受度。
news.Keywords: ETF, 市值型, 不配息, 資本利得, 動能策略

news.Title: 郭明錤稱聯發科AI定位升級，鎖定Google TPU與馬斯克L10機櫃商機
news.Content: 【財訊快報／陳孟朔】天風國際證券分析師郭明錤發表的最新產業調查指出，聯發科(2454)內部已將AI事業策略定位，從「IC ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 分析師郭明錤指出，聯發科(2454)已將AI事業策略定位從「IC/ASIC設計」提升至「系統級別設計」。
• 聯發科的首要目標鎖定Google TPU的PCBA(L6)以及馬斯克旗下公司自研AI晶片的L10機櫃。
• 此轉變意味聯發科將延伸至系統整合、驗證與高附加價值設計服務，而非僅止於晶片設計。
• 聯發科預計採取「主導設計與驗證」的輕資產模式，毛利率目標40%至50%，並善用台灣硬體供應鏈優勢。
• 聯發科在馬斯克旗下公司機櫃設計方面有潛在機會，因其組裝生態尚未完備。
news.Keywords: 聯發科, AI策略, 系統級設計, Google TPU, L10機櫃

news.Title: Why Summer Is the Best Time to Review Your Estate Plan
news.Content: Although not the first person to make this kind of joke, in 1789 Benjamin Franklin told Jean-Baptiste Le Roy: "In this world nothing can be said to be certain, except death and taxes." The joke was meant to be about taxes, but the inevitability of death is just as important when you are looking at estate planning. Here's why this summer could be the best time for you to review your estate plan. Nobody wants to contemplate their own demise Deep down inside, we ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article discusses the importance of reviewing estate plans, especially during summer.
• It uses Benjamin Franklin's quote about death and taxes to introduce the topic.
• The content touches upon the emotional aspects of estate planning and involving heirs.
• A brief, unrelated mention of a 'Double Down' signal for Nvidia in 2009 is included.
news.Keywords: estate planning, taxes, heirs, financial planning, summer

news.Title: 聯發科不只做晶片了！ 郭明錤爆料：盯上馬斯克的「大機櫃」
news.Content: 天風國際證券分析師郭明錤表示，根據最新的產業調查顯示，聯發科內部已將 AI事業的策略定位，從「IC / ASIC 設計」提升至「系統級別設計」，首要目標鎖定G ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 分析師郭明錤指出，聯發科(2454)已將AI事業策略定位從「IC/ASIC設計」提升至「系統級別設計」。
• 聯發科的首要目標鎖定Google TPU的PCBA(L6)以及馬斯克旗下公司自研AI晶片的L10機櫃。
• 此轉變意味聯發科將延伸至系統整合、驗證與高附加價值設計服務，而非僅止於晶片設計。
• 聯發科預計採取「主導設計與驗證」的輕資產模式，毛利率目標40%至50%，並善用台灣硬體供應鏈優勢。
• 聯發科在馬斯克旗下公司機櫃設計方面有潛在機會，因其組裝生態尚未完備。
news.Keywords: 聯發科, AI策略, 系統級設計, Google TPU, L10機櫃

news.Title: SpaceX吸走56%散戶買盤！科技「七巨頭」升級「FAB 10」 OpenAI、Anthropic將入列
news.Content: SpaceX(SPCX-US) 上周五掛牌上市，引爆散戶投資人瘋狂搶進。分析師認為，這不僅是今年最受矚目的 IPO，也可能象徵科技產業投資版圖正式改寫，市場正從過去的「科技七巨頭」(Magnificent Seven) 邁向新的「FA ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX 上市首日吸引了 1.17 億美元的散戶買盤，佔當天美股散戶個股買盤總額的 56%。
• 散戶投資人在此次 IPO 中獲得約 20% 的配售額，高於一般 IPO 水準。
• 市場預期 SpaceX、OpenAI 和 Anthropic 將共同構成新的「FAB 10」時代，代表未來 10 年 AI 與科技產業的發展方向。
• SpaceX 當天的成交量超越了輝達、蘋果、微軟、特斯拉、Meta 與 Google 的總和，顯示資金正向少數超大型科技企業集中。
• SpaceX 的熱潮可能正在吸走原本流向其他熱門科技股（如半導體族群）的資金。
news.Keywords: IPO, 散戶買盤, 科技巨頭, AI, 資金流向

news.Title: 華為沒有EUV拚出麒麟9030！研調拆解晶片 揭突破美國封鎖3難關
news.Content: 大陸通訊設備龍頭華為於5月底提出「韜定律」概念，宣示目標在2031年前打造出相當於1.4奈米製程水準的晶片。然而，半導體研究機構《SemiAnalysi ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 華為最新推出的麒麟 9030 Pro 處理器採用中芯國際（SMIC）的 N+3 製程技術，大致相當於台積電 N6 製程。
• 為達成此製程，中芯國際需大量依賴深紫外光（DUV）多重曝光技術，而非主流的極紫外光（EUV）設備，增加了生產成本並影響良率。
• 麒麟 9030 Pro 的整體性能約相當於三年前的安卓旗艦晶片，與目前最新旗艦處理器相比仍有差距，尤其在功耗與能源效率方面。
• 美國的半導體出口管制迫使中國企業採取不同技術路線，中芯國際透過 DUV 多重曝光、DTCO 及複雜晶片整合技術來提升效能。
• 儘管中芯國際能生產出接近台積電規格的晶片，但製造成本高出數倍且不良品比例較高，在製程成熟度、經濟效益與量產能力上仍難與台積電相比。
news.Keywords: 先進製程, 中芯國際, 華為, EUV, DUV

news.Title: Could SpaceX Be More Valuable Than Amazon and Microsoft? Here's What Price Its Stock Would Need to Hit for That to Happen
news.Content: At over $2.5 trillion in market cap, SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) is already one of the most valuable companies in the world right now. It's more highly valued than blue chip companies such as Coca-Cola , JPMorgan , and McDonald's , which, unlike SpaceX, are highly profitable businesses. Given ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's market capitalization has reached over $2.5 trillion, surpassing established companies like Coca-Cola and JPMorgan.
• Its valuation is approaching that of tech giants Amazon ($2.6T) and Microsoft ($3T), despite incurring billions in losses and having significantly lower revenue.
• The stock is trading at over 100 times revenue with a growth rate of only 15% last quarter, indicating a highly speculative investment.
• Analysts warn of significant downside risk due to its current valuation, lack of profitability, and substantial investments in AI.
news.Keywords: spacex, valuation, market cap, speculative, growth

news.Title: Intel gets a $170 billion AI reason to matter again
news.Content: For much of the artificial intelligence boom, investors have heard one simple story. Graphics processing units were at the center of it all. That thesis made Nvidia ( NVDA ) a must-have in the stock market. It also drove investors to Advanced Micro Devices ( AMD ) , Broadcom ( AVGO ) , Marvell Technology ( MRVL ), and other companies involved in the data-center buildout. But Bank of America ( BAC ) argues the next chapter of AI could give a forgotten corner of the semiconductor market a major boost. Central processing units, or CPUs, could be significantly more ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Bank of America (BofA) believes 'agentic AI' could significantly boost the server CPU market, potentially reaching $170 billion by 2030.
• BofA upgraded Intel (INTC) to 'buy' from 'underperform' and raised its price target to $135, citing the potential for CPUs to become more crucial in advanced AI systems.
• This shift in AI evolution could provide Intel with a renewed opportunity, addressing past manufacturing delays and market-share erosion.
• While GPUs remain critical for training, agentic AI's sequential and latency-sensitive tasks could increase the importance and demand for CPUs, benefiting companies like Intel and potentially AMD.
news.Keywords: agentic ai, cpu market, intel, semiconductor, bank of america

Frank Holmes is Executive Chairman of HIVE Digital Technologies, the first Bitcoin miner to ...
news.AI_Summary:

Today, its market cap reached $2.40 trillion, making it the sixth-most-valuable company in the world. That puts it ahead of tech giants like Broadcom , Meta , and Tesla , but could it overtake Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) -- which ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's IPO valued the company at $1.77 trillion, reaching $2.40 trillion and surpassing Broadcom, Meta, and Tesla in market cap, but trailing Amazon.
• SpaceX reported a 33% revenue increase to $18.67 billion in 2025 but incurred a net loss of $4.94 billion, with significant AI investments expected to keep it unprofitable.
• The company's market cap is 128 times its 2025 revenue, indicating a high valuation compared to growth stock metrics and peers like Rocket Lab and AST SpaceMobile.
• Amazon, valued at three times its current sales and 28 times earnings, is expected to grow revenue and EPS at CAGRs of 14% and 21% respectively, driven by e-commerce and cloud.
• Analysts project Amazon's market cap could reach $3.76 trillion within 12 months, suggesting SpaceX's current valuation is unsustainable and likely to decrease.
news.Keywords: IPO valuation, market capitalization, profitability concerns, revenue growth, competitive landscape

news.Title: Stocks Settle Sharply Higher as US-Iran Peace Deal Spurs Optimism
news.Content:
The S&P 500 Index ($SPX) (SPY) on Monday closed up +1.65%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average ($DOWI) (DIA) closed up +0.92%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index ($IUXX) (QQQ) closed up +3.06%. June E-mini S&P futures (ESM26) rose +1.68%, and June E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQM26) rose +3.06%.

Stock indexes settled sharply higher on Monday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 posting 1-week highs, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average hitting a new record high. Crude oil prices and global bond yields tumbled on Monday, sparking risk-on sentiment and boosting equity markets after the US and Iran agreed to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump said the Strait of Hormuz will reopen after the signing of the peace deal on Friday in Switzerland, which will trigger the start of 60 days of talks on Iran's nuclear program. However, if an agreement isn't reached on nuclear, the US could restart military attacks.

More News from Barchart

Dear Rocket Lab Stock Fans, Mark Your Calendars for June 22

Stay Away from Super Micro Computer Stock. It’s Hostage to Its Own Mistakes.

Qualcomm Is Striving for a Bigger Piece of AI Infrastructure. Here’s What That Means for QCOM Stock.

Get exclusive insights with the FREE Barchart Brief newsletter. Subscribe now for quick, incisive midday market analysis you won't find anywhere else.

Gains in technology stocks led the overall market higher on Monday. Also, airline stocks rose as the slump in crude oil lowers fuel costs, and metal prices and mining stocks rallied as the drop in oil prices is dovish for the world’s central banks.

On the downside, falling crude prices weighed on energy producers. Also, Monday’s weaker-than-expected US economic reports on the June Empire manufacturing survey, May manufacturing production, and the June NAHB housing market index were bearish for stocks.

The US Jun Empire manufacturing survey of general business conditions fell -13.9 to 5.7, weaker than expectations of 13.7.

US May manufacturing production was unchanged m/m, weaker than expectations of +0.3% m/m.

The US Jun NAHB housing market index unexpectedly fell -2 to 35, weaker than expectations of no change at 37.

WTI crude oil prices (CLN26) fell more than -4% on Monday to a 3-month low on news of the end of the US-Iran war and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The plunge in oil prices has eased inflation expectations and lowered global bond yields. The 10-year T-note yield dropped to a 1-month low of 4.42% on Monday.

Story Continues

The markets are discounting a 4% chance of a +25 bp rate hike at the next FOMC meeting on June 16-17.

Overseas stock markets settled higher on Monday. The Euro Stoxx 50 rose to a new record high and closed up +0.68%. China's Shanghai Composite climbed to a 1.5-week high and closed up +1.61%. Japan’s Nikkei-225 Stock Average surged to a new all-time high and closed up +4.99%.

Interest Rates

September 10-year T-notes (ZNU6) on Monday closed up +4.5 ticks, and the 10-year T-note yield fell -1.6 bp to 4.463%. Sep T-notes rallied to a 1-month high on Monday, and the 10-year T-note yield dropped to a 1-month low of 4.418%. Monday’s -4% plunge in WTI crude oil to a 3-month low lowered inflation expectations and is bullish for T-notes. Also, Monday’s weaker-than-expected US economic reports were supportive for T-notes. Gains in T-notes were limited as Monday’s sharp stock market rally dampened safe-haven demand for government debt securities.

European government bond yields moved lower on Monday. The 10-year German bund yield tumbled to a 2-week low of 2.944% and finished down -4.1 bp to 2.954%. The 10-year UK gilt yield dropped to a 1.75-month low of 4.767% and finished down -2.4 bp to 4.812%.

Eurozone Apr industrial production rose +0.1% m/m, right on expectations.

ECB Governing Council member Martins Kazaks said, "The ECB raised the inflation forecast quite significantly, but the risks in my view are still on the upside for inflation," so the ECB is ready to take further action to stop the rise in energy prices from spreading to the rest of the economy.

Swaps are discounting an 18% chance of a +25 bp ECB rate hike at its next policy meeting on July 23.

US Stock Movers

Chipmakers and AI infrastructure stocks rose on Monday, helping support gains in the broader market. The iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX) rose more than +5% on Monday to a new record high. Western Digital (WDC) closed up more than +15% to lead gainers in the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100, and Marvell Technology (MRVL) closed up more than +11%. Also, Micron Technology (MU) closed up more than +10%, and Seagate Technology Holdings Plc (STX) closed up more than +8%. In addition, ARM Holdings Plc (ARM) closed up more than +7%, and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Lam Research (LRCX) closed up more than +6%. Finally, SanDisk (SNDK) and Microchip Technology (MCHP) closed up more than +5%, and Qualcomm (QCOM) and Texas Instruments (TXN) closed up more than +4%.

The Magnificent Seven technology stocks rallied on Monday as the end of the US-Iran war has sparked risk-on sentiment in asset markets. Meta Platforms (META) closed up more than +4%, and Amazon.com (AMZN) and Nvidia (NVDA) closed up more than +3%. Also, Alphabet (GOOGL) and Microsoft (MSFT) closed up more than +2%, and Apple (AAPL) closed up more than +1%. In addition, Tesla (TSLA) closed up +0.74%.

Airline stocks and cruise line operators are rallying today as the -5% plunge in WTI crude lowers fuel costs and boosts the profitability prospects for the companies. Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) closed up more than +6%, and United Airlines Holdings (UAL), Carnival (CCL), Alaska Air Group (ALK), Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings (NCLH), and American Airlines Group (AAL) closed up more than +3%. In addition, Delta Air Lines (DAL) and Southwest Airlines (LUV) closed up more than +1%.

Mining stocks moved higher on Monday as gold, silver, and copper prices rallied sharply. Coeur Mining (CDE) and Hecla Mining (HL) closed up more than +8%, and Anglogold Ashanti (AU) closed up more than +6%. Also, Newmont Corp (NEM) closed up more than +5%, and Barrick Mining (B) closed up more than +4%. In addition, Freeport McMoRan (FCX) closed up more than +2%, and Southern Copper (SCCO) closed up more than +1%.

Cryptocurrency-exposed stocks gained on Monday as the price of Bitcoin (^BTCUSD) rose more than +4% to a 1.5-week high. Coinbase Global (COIN) closed up more than +6%, and Strategy (MSTR) closed up more than +5%. Also, MARA Holdings (MARA) closed up more than +3%, and. Riot Platforms (RIOT) closed up more than +2%.

Delivery and rideshare service stocks settled higher on Monday as gasoline prices tumbled to a 2-month low. DoorDash (DASH) closed up more than +11%, and Uber Technologies (UBER) and Lyft (LYFT) closed up by more than +5%. Also, Grab Holdings Ltd (GRAB) closed up by more than +4%, and Maplebear (CART) closed up by more than +2%.

Energy stocks and service providers retreated on Monday as WTI crude oil fell more than -4% to a 3-month low. APA Corp (APA) closed down more than -6%, and Marathon Petroleum (MPC), SLB Ltd (SLB), Exxon Mobil (XOM), ConocoPhillips (COP), and Valero Energy (VLO) closed down more than -4%. Also, Chevron (CVX) closed down more than -3% to lead losers in the Dow Jones Industrials, and Phillips 66 (PSX), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), Haliburton (HAL), and Devon Energy (DVN) closed down more than -3%.

Space Exploration Technologies (SPCX) closed up more than +19% on positive carryover from its record $75 billion initial public offering (IPO) late last week, which was more than four times oversubscribed, indicating strong demand for the stock.

Datadog (DDOG) closed up more than +1% after Truist Securities upgraded the stock to buy from hold with a price target of $300.

Hawkeye 360 Inc (HAWK) closed up more than +5% after Jeffries upgraded the stock to buy from hold with a price target of $34.

Elicio Therapeutics (ELTX) closed down more than -72% after it said its Phase 2 study of its vaccine to treat patients with pancreatic cancer did not meet the pre-specified primary endpoint.

Fox Corp (FOXA) closed down more than -16% to lead losers in the S&P 500 after paying $22 billion to acquire Roku.

Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) closed down more than -3% to lead losers in the Nasdaq 100 after Citigroup downgraded the stock to sell from neutral with a price target of $228.

Earnings Reports(6/16/2026)

John Wiley & Sons Inc (WLY) and La-Z-Boy Inc (LZB).

news.Title: This analyst sees Intel reaching a $5 trillion valuation
news.Content: Investing.com - Global Equities Research has published a sweeping bull thesis on Intel (INTC), setting a $200 near-term price target and projecting the chipmaker will eventually reach a $5 trillion market capitalization. The current market cap sits around $640 billion. Analyst Trip Chowdhry argues that a structural shift in AI computing from training to inference is about to put Intel at the center of the technology industry, though ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Global Equities Research has initiated a bullish thesis on Intel (INTC), projecting a potential $5 trillion market capitalization from its current $640 billion.
• The analyst believes a shift from AI training to AI inference and applications will position Intel's CPUs, particularly 18A Xeon and Panther Lake processors, as central to future computing.
• The thesis highlights Intel's potential leadership in Edge AI, with claims of 18A Panther Lake laptops running large LLMs and Intel holding a significant process technology lead over TSMC.
• The report cites potential foundry customers including Apple, Google, Amazon, and Microsoft, with claims of Apple using Intel's 14A process, though these are unconfirmed.
• Projections include 2030 EPS of $10 and a $200 price target, emphasizing Intel's role in the 'Angstrom Era' of AI.
news.Keywords: AI inference, market cap projection, foundry services, process technology, CPU vs GPU

news.Title: Why Tesla Might Need to Merge With SpaceX Sooner Rather Than Later

Both companies are run by Elon Musk, they work on overlapping projects, and ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Rumors of a potential merger between Elon Musk-led SpaceX and Tesla are intensifying following SpaceX's IPO.
• Tesla's growth narrative may be less appealing due to increased EV competition and potential margin pressure, alongside SpaceX offering more compelling opportunities in space and AI.
• Tesla's stock has declined 9% year-to-date, potentially motivating a merger to pool resources and reduce competition for investor capital.
• A merger could occur as early as next year, driven by Tesla's eroding competitive position and SpaceX's need for capital to fund growth.
• The companies are collaborating on projects like a giant chip factory (Terafab), suggesting potential synergies from a combination.
news.Keywords: merger speculation, strategic combination, EV market competition, AI growth narrative, capital allocation

The company is marketing bonds in seven parts, with maturities running from two years to 30 years, according to Bloomberg. It would be Nvidia's first corporate bond ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia plans to raise at least $20 billion in the bond market, marking its first corporate bond sale since 2021.
• This move is part of a broader trend of companies, including Alphabet ($80 billion stock sale), tapping capital markets to fund AI build-outs.
• The AI trade is increasingly becoming a funding story, with significant capital expenditures required for data centers, chips, and infrastructure.
• While Nvidia's bond proceeds are for general corporate purposes, it signifies participation in the large-scale AI financing wave.
news.Keywords: debt issuance, AI financing, capital expenditures, bond market, funding wave

news.Title: Kalshi Traders See 49% Chance SpaceX and Tesla Will Merge Within a Year
news.Content: The SpaceX IPO is now behind us, but we may not be done with big news from Elon Musk. Traders on prediction market Kalshi are pricing a 49% chance that Space Exploration Technologies (NASDAQ:SPCX) will merge with electric vehicle maker Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) before May 1, 2027. As both companies are headed by Musk and rely heavily on artificial intelligence, there’s long been speculation that the two companies could eventually merge. CNBC reported on May 27 that Tesla and SpaceX were already considering a merger, and Musk himself has reportedly ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Traders on Kalshi are pricing a 49% chance of a merger between SpaceX and Tesla within a year.
• Both companies are led by Elon Musk and heavily rely on artificial intelligence, fueling speculation.

news.Keywords: merger speculation, AI synergies, valuation, competitive landscape, EV market

• Ambarella (AMBA), a lesser-known semiconductor company, is well-positioned for the edge AI market, which is expected to grow from $25 billion in 2025 to $119 billion by 2033.

news.Title: NIO & TSLA See Fresh Tailwinds But Only One Stock Is Worth Owning
news.Content: Two of the electric vehicle (EV) world's most-watched names are in the spotlight and for the right reasons. NIO Inc. NIO and Tesla TSLA have both had their share of turbulence. news.Title: Microsoft CEO sends a blunt warning on AI and the tech ecosystem
news.Content: For most of the past three years, the AI investment story has been simple. Build the biggest model, train it on the most data, and the market rewards you. On June 14, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella published a lengthy post arguing that story is about to change, and that investors who keep following it could end up backing the wrong companies entirely. His argument is not that AI is moving too slowly. It is that the industry may be building toward an outcome that, in his own words, "the political economy will simply not tolerate." The two kinds of capital ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns that the current AI investment narrative, focused solely on building larger models, is unsustainable and could lead to market intolerance.
• He emphasizes the importance of both human capital and 'token capital' (proprietary AI systems) for companies.
• Nadella expresses concern about AI concentration, where a few foundation models capture most of the economic value, potentially hollowing out industries.
• He draws a parallel to globalization's impact, where aggregate GDP growth masked real displacement, suggesting AI's aggregate numbers could also be misleading.
• Microsoft's strategy focuses on platforms and infrastructure built on top of foundation models, allowing companies to swap underlying models without losing proprietary knowledge.
news.Keywords: AI concentration, Human capital, Token capital, Regulatory risk, Platform strategy

news.Title: After a Record-Breaking Debut, Is There Still Room to Run in SpaceX?
news.Content: Some companies go public. SpaceX made history. When shares of Elon Musk’s rocket-and-satellite empire began trading on the Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX last Friday, they did so as the largest initial public offering the world has ever seen. Priced at $135 per share, the deal valued the company at roughly $1.77 trillion — larger than Tesla on day one. news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's IPO was the largest in history, valuing the company at $1.77 trillion and opening at $150 per share, a 19.2% gain on the IPO price.

• Starlink is projected to generate $15.5 billion to $20 billion in revenue in 2026 and turned free-cash-flow positive in 2024.
• SpaceX's launch capabilities, with internal Falcon 9 costs between $15 million and $30 million, provide a significant cost advantage.

news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced what he described as a “full ban” on children using social media, going beyond limits imposed by Australia last year to create one of the strictest online crackdowns in the democratic world. news.Title: 5 Potential Buyers of Roku That Actually Make Sense

The company behind the country's most popular TV streaming operating system jumped 20% after sources told Bloomberg Roku was in talks with at least one media company for a potential sale. Roku doesn't need to be bailed out. It's growing faster than it has in several years. It's been consistently profitable over the past year, and its balance sheet is flush with more than $2 billion in cash and no long-term debt. It shouldn't be desperate, giving it more leverage than a typical ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Roku is experiencing strong growth, is consistently profitable, and has a healthy balance sheet with over $2 billion in cash and no long-term debt.
• Potential acquirers identified include Comcast (CMCSA), Microsoft (MSFT), Netflix (NFLX), The Trade Desk (TTD), and Disney (DIS).
• Microsoft's acquisition of Roku would position it as a leader in TV operating systems and streaming devices, leveraging its significant financial resources.

news.Title: Stock Market Investors Get an Urgent Warning From the Bond Market. History Says This Will Happen Next.
news.Content: The U.S. stock market has been rocketing higher despite economic uncertainty created by the Iran war. Since April, the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) and the Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) have advanced 13% and 19%, respectively, amid a wave of strong financial results. However, investors got an urgent warning from the bond market last month. The 30-year Treasury yield hit a 19-year high, hinting ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The bond market is sending an urgent warning to stock investors, with the 30-year Treasury yield hitting a 19-year high.
• This spike in yields, driven by inflation concerns and potential Fed rate hikes, historically preceded significant stock market declines (e.g., S&P 500 down 21%, Nasdaq down 18% in 2009).
• High fuel prices are contributing to core inflation, with core PCE hitting 3.3%, increasing the likelihood of Fed rate hikes rather than cuts.
• Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase warn that rising bond yields and inflation could trigger a stock market correction or even a recession.
• Investors are now betting on a quarter-point rate increase later this year, a shift from earlier expectations of rate cuts.
news.Keywords: bond yields, inflation, Federal Reserve, interest rates, market correction

news.Title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Hit $1 Trillion in Record Time. Wall Street Says This Will Happen Next.
news.Content: In May, Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) became the 12th U.S. company to achieve a $1 trillion market value, and the memory-chip maker reached the milestone in record time. After hitting $500 billion earlier this year, Micron soared to $1 trillion in just 48 days. Before this year, Tesla held the record at 230 days. And it took Nvidia nearly 500 days. But an unprecedented ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology achieved a $1 trillion market value in record time (48 days), surpassing Tesla and Nvidia.
• Samsung and SK Hynix also saw their market values double rapidly due to an unprecedented memory chip supply shortage.
• Micron's memory chips (NAND and DRAM, including HBM) are crucial for AI infrastructure, feeding data to GPUs at high speeds.
• Despite strong recent performance (revenue up 196% to $23.8 billion, non-GAAP net income up 682% to $12.20/share), the driving force was price increases due to shortage.
• Analysts are cautious, with a median target price implying 15% downside, and anticipate supply will eventually overtake demand within three years, potentially causing prices to crater.
news.Keywords: market capitalization, memory chips, supply shortage, AI infrastructure, cyclical market

news.Title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh and the FOMC Will Likely Take the First Step Toward Dropping the Hammer on Trumpflation This Week
news.Content: It's been an eventful past month on Wall Street. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) , S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) , and Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) jumped to fresh highs, while a changing of the guard was made official at the Federal Reserve. Jerome Powell served his final day as Fed chair on May 15 , while Trump's handpicked ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Federal Reserve, under new Chair Kevin Warsh, is expected to shift from an easing bias to a neutral stance on interest rates.
• This shift is anticipated due to rising inflation (4.2% trailing 12-month high) and a majority of FOMC members opposing the previous easing bias.
• A neutral bias would provide the FOMC more flexibility to raise rates, which could be perceived negatively by the stock market.
• The market's reliance on debt for AI data center build-outs makes it vulnerable to potential rate hikes, suggesting this policy shift could negatively impact stock valuations.
news.Keywords: Monetary Policy Shift, Interest Rates, Inflation, FOMC, Market Impact

news.Title: SpaceX Climbed Nearly 20% in Its First Day of Trading. Here's Where the Stock Price Will Be in 3 Months, According to History.
news.Content: SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) advanced more than 19% on Friday, its first day of trading -- and reached a market value of $2.1 trillion. This immediately puts it in the league of the world's biggest tech companies, such as Apple and Microsoft , in the so-called "trillion-dollar" club. SpaceX set its IPO price at $135, the stock opened at $150, and it closed at more than $160. The IPO offers SpaceX a spot in the record books, as it raised $75 billion for the biggest IPO ever. It isn't uncommon for a stock to soar ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX (SPCX) experienced a significant IPO, rising nearly 20% on its first day to a market value of $2.1 trillion, placing it among tech giants like Apple and Microsoft.
• The company raised $75 billion in its IPO, the largest ever, with its stock opening at $150 and closing above $160.
• SpaceX's business segments include rocket launches, satellite connectivity (Starlink, which generated $4.4 billion in income last year with 120% YoY growth), and AI.
• Despite strong growth potential, SpaceX faces high investment requirements and risks associated with new technologies, having reported an annual loss of $4.9 billion last year.
news.Keywords: IPO Performance, Market Valuation, Starlink Growth, AI Business, Investment Risk

news.Title: Exclusive-Tesla presented misleading ‘Full Self-Driving’ safety data to European regulators
news.Content: By Chris Kirkham and Marie Mannes June 15 (Reuters) - In its efforts to secure European approval of its “Full Self-Driving” (FSD) system, Tesla has presented self-published safety statistics to regulators in Sweden and the Netherlands that independent traffic-safety researchers have said amount to misleading marketing. More from Yahoo Scout What is Tesla's FSD approval status in Europe? What safety statistics did Tesla present to European regulators? Why is European FSD approval important for Tesla's business? How are researchers challenging Tesla's FSD safety claims? A Reuters examination published last month found that Tesla CEO Elon Musk and other leaders over ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Tesla allegedly presented misleading safety data regarding its 'Full Self-Driving' (FSD) system to European regulators in Sweden and the Netherlands.
• Independent researchers claim Tesla's self-published safety statistics exaggerate the system's capabilities, using invalid comparisons.
• Tesla is seeking European approval for FSD, a subscription-based driver-assistance feature, to regain market share in the region.
• The Dutch regulator (RDW) approved FSD in April, stating they perform their own tests and do not solely rely on marketing claims.
• Concerns include comparing airbag-triggering crashes to all vehicle crashes and using older average US vehicles as a benchmark.
news.Keywords: Autonomous Driving, Regulatory Scrutiny, Safety Data, Misleading Claims, Market Share

news.Title: Anthropic Block Marks US Reversal, Warning to Silicon Valley
news.Content: (Bloomberg) -- The extraordinary move by the US to bar foreign access to Anthropic PBC’s best AI models underscores the Trump administration’s newfound willingness to exert control over a pivotal industry. It also reminds Silicon Valley that it’s working with an imperfectly understood technology with uncertain impact. Most Read from Bloomberg Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Confirms Deal Reached With US Iran Signals No Deal Will Be Signed by Trump’s Sunday Timeline US and Iran Agree to Halt War, Restart Middle East Oil Shipments Why Musk Raced to Take SpaceX Public in the World’s Biggest IPO Iran Pushes ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The US government has ordered Anthropic PBC to disable access to its advanced AI models for foreign nationals due to security concerns.
• This unprecedented move follows the discovery that Anthropic's Fable 5 AI model could be 'jailbroken'.
• The action raises concerns about potential impacts on future AI model deployments and sets a precedent for AI companies like OpenAI, Google, and Meta.
• The US government's intervention signifies a shift towards treating frontier AI models as strategic assets controlled by national security considerations.
news.Keywords: AI regulation, national security, AI model access, government intervention, frontier models

news.Title: 追指數已褪流行？主動式台股ETF規模狂飆5倍 跨國主動股票ETF也暴增779億

news.Title: Tata's iPhone parts factory contaminated farmland water, India pollution body alleges
news.Content: By Munsif Vengattil and Aditya Kalra BENGALURU, June 13 (Reuters) - An Indian pollution regulator has alleged wastewater discharged from a Tata components factory for Apple 's iPhone has contaminated the groundwater for nearby farms and warned of a ‌forced shutdown unless Tata gives a satisfactory explanation. More from Yahoo Scout What challenges has Apple's India supply ...
news.AI_Summary:
• An Indian pollution regulator alleges that wastewater from a Tata factory supplying Apple's iPhone has contaminated groundwater.
• The regulator has warned of a forced shutdown for the Tata plant in Hosur unless a satisfactory explanation is provided.
• Farmland owners complained about contamination of land and wells, leading to inspections that found wastewater overflow from a factory pond.
• Tata Electronics claims to be in full compliance with all regulatory norms, based on an independent analysis, while Apple and the Tamil Nadu government have not commented.

news.Content:

中東局勢出現重大轉折，伊朗政府15日宣布，已與美國達成協議，雙方衝突將立即停止，並在各戰線實 ...
news.AI_Summary:

• 消息曝光後，亞洲市場情緒樂觀，日本、南韓股市同步大漲，日經指數盤中漲幅一度超過4%，南韓股市勁揚逾5%。

news.Title: 買房收租還是存台股？
news.Content:
面對根深柢固「有土斯有財」的想法，讓許多台灣人深信，房地產是累積財富最低風險的捷徑。但攤開真實數據，真的是這樣嗎？

\n

買房好，還是把錢投入股市與退休金好？在台灣，如果你把這個問題拋到家庭聚餐的餐桌上，多數長輩與同儕的答案 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 比較過去20年數據，台股含息總報酬率超過600%，遠優於房地產的約3倍漲幅。
• 假設2006年投入1000萬，台股資產約可達4500-5000萬，房產約2800萬（未計租金）。
• 台灣房地產淨租金收益率僅2-3%，扣除成本後更低，遠不如台股殖利率。
• 分析房地產槓桿風險、政策變數、區域風險及流動性差等缺點，建議將資金透過主動型基金投資台灣優質企業。
news.Keywords: 資產配置,長期投資,報酬率比較,房地產,股市

news.Title: Apple's iOS 27 surprise could change the AI narrative
news.Content: AI is now the largest battleground in technology. Over the last two years investors have pumped hundreds of billions of dollars into firms promising to lead the next stage of the AI revolution. At Apple ( AAPL ) , that talk has become more and more about the iPhone. Many experts have said AI might fuel the company's next major upgrade cycle, when millions of customers replace outdated devices with newer versions that can run advanced capabilities. That’s why one of Apple’s less-covered announcements at its Worldwide Developers Conference may be worthy of more attention than its other headline-grabbing AI ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple's iOS 27 will support existing devices, including the iPhone 11, potentially impacting the expected AI-driven iPhone upgrade cycle.
• While the iPhone remains Apple's largest business, the company's growth is increasingly reliant on its digital ecosystem and recurring service revenue.
• Apple's AI strategy may prioritize deepening customer relationships and ecosystem engagement over immediate hardware upgrades.
• This approach suggests a focus on slower, more enduring growth rather than a short-term AI supercycle driven by new device sales.
news.Keywords: Ecosystem Strategy, Recurring Revenue, AI Integration, Customer Retention, Hardware Upgrades

news.Title: How ‘agentic’ is AI shopping today
news.Content: Investing.com -- Agentic shopping is the use of AI agents to carry out parts of the shopping process on behalf of the customer instead of merely providing recommendations. Google’s agentic shopping protocol, for instance, enables agents to work across different parts of the buying processes, including discovery and post-purchase support. But ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Agentic shopping, using AI agents for tasks like product discovery and payment, is still in early stages and requires human intervention for purchases.
• A test of AI shopping tools (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Alexa, Sparky) revealed that while foundational models offer broad search, they lack retail-specific data.
• Retail-native AI agents provide precise results but are limited to their own product assortments.
• Bridging the gap between general AI and retail-specific AI, particularly in payment and fulfillment, is crucial for true end-to-end agentic e-commerce.
news.Keywords: AI Agents, E-commerce, Shopping Automation, Foundational Models, Retail Technology

news.Title: Millions of Americans Are Retiring Earlier Than They'd Planned To -- Here's Why
news.Content: What's your plan for retirement? Every investor should have a solid plan in place for what happens when the work ends, which includes taking the time to study their expenses, estimate how much income they'll need in retirement, and, importantly, figure out how they'll get that income. But as the Scottish poet Robert Burns noted in a poem, "The best laid schemes o' Mice an' Men, / Gang aft agley" -- that is, our plans often ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Millions of Americans are retiring earlier than planned, often due to health issues or job loss, according to a study by Allianz Life.
• Retiring early can lead to financial challenges, including lost savings, reduced investment growth, and increased healthcare costs.
• The article advises proactive retirement planning, including saving aggressively and considering strategies to avoid early retirement.
• It also mentions potential Social Security benefits and strategies to maximize retirement income.
news.Keywords: Retirement Planning, Financial Security, Early Retirement, Social Security, Longevity Risk

The new multi-year collaboration centers on Design Technology Co-Optimization (DTCO) for Intel’s upcoming process nodes, beginning with Intel 14A, and brings together Cadence’s agentic AI-powered EDA and Design IP solutions with Intel’s advanced manufacturing expertise. The partnership is focused on optimizing tools, design flows, and methodologies to deliver industry-leading performance, power, and area (PPA) improvements. As part of the effort, Cadence ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Cadence Design Systems (CDNS) announced an expanded partnership with Intel Foundry focused on Design Technology Co-Optimization (DTCO) for Intel's upcoming process nodes, starting with Intel 14A.
• The collaboration aims to optimize tools, design flows, and methodologies for industry-leading performance, power, and area (PPA) improvements.
• Cadence's agentic AI-powered EDA and Design IP solutions will be integrated with Intel's manufacturing expertise.
• Cadence's stock rose 4.8% on June 8 following the partnership announcement.
• Cadence reported Q1 2026 revenue of $1.47 billion, up 19% year-over-year, and non-GAAP EPS of $1.96, up 25% year-over-year.
news.Keywords: semiconductor, EDA, AI, strategic partnership, earnings growth

news.Title: Xbox fans waiting on Project Helix get unexpected news from Wedbush
news.Content: The explosion in AI spending is having a ripple effect throughout the tech industry, and now gamers are going to pay the price. To find out just how much weight AI holds in the U.S. economy, the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis looked at how much money is being spent on software, R&D and information processing equipment, along with data-center construction data. In the first quarter last year, information processing equipment, which includes microchips like those made by Nvidia ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI spending is causing a hardware component crisis for Xbox, with costs for console storage components quadrupling and projected to be five times higher by 2027.
• This crisis could lead to console shortages as far out as the 2027 holiday season, impacting the launch of Project Helix.
• Wedbush analysts note that memory/NAND shortages are significantly impacting consumer hardware economics, adding substantial cost to console storage.
• Xbox is planning layoffs and budget cuts for marketing and other areas as part of a business reorganization.
news.Keywords: AI spending, hardware component crisis, console shortages, memory/NAND, Xbox

news.Title: Corning’s Amazon Deal Validates the AI Optics Boom. What That Means for GLW Stock.
news.Content: The AI Infrastructure trade has evolved beyond chips. Corning's (GLW) stock surged after it signed a multibillion-dollar multidecade contract with Amazon (AMZN) to ramp up U.S. production of fiber optics for data centers. This contract is expected to generate thousands of jobs, with 1,000 positions alone created by Corning in North Carolina, along with many construction jobs and an employee training program. It is important because, besides ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Corning (GLW) secured a multibillion-dollar, multidecade contract with Amazon (AMZN) to increase U.S. production of fiber optics for data centers.
• The deal is expected to create thousands of jobs and validates the growing demand for AI optics and advanced connectivity.
• Corning's Optical Communications segment saw sales growth of 36% in Q1 2026.
• Corning reported Q1 2026 core sales of $4.35 billion, up 18% YoY, and core EPS of $0.70, up 30% YoY.
• Corning also announced two additional hyperscaler contracts worth up to $6 billion each, similar to its previous deal with Meta (META).
news.Keywords: fiber optics, data center, AI infrastructure, supply chain, contract win

Hence, companies that provide the power and ...
news.AI_Summary:

• Bloom Energy's solid oxide fuel cells are becoming crucial for data centers facing power shortages and grid delays, with Oracle's AI factory to use its servers.

• Oneok, a midstream energy company, is seeing increased relevance as data centers demand more natural gas-fired power, with advanced discussions for significant potential natural gas demand.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, data center power, energy demand, fuel cells, natural gas

news.Title: 預言台股下周「洗盤可能性非常高」！分析師點高風險股：極好的布局時間點
news.Content: 受美國通膨數據、國際局勢等因素影響，台股本周劇烈震盪，對此，摩爾投顧分析師謝晨彥在節目 《財經一路發》 表示，當前每日盤勢變化劇烈，台股、美股的平均真實波幅（Average True Range）均面臨大幅度攀升，按照過去經驗，當股市 ...

news.Keywords: 市場波動,洗盤機率,AI趨勢,財報預期,布局時點

news.Title: The Most Dangerous Dividend ETF Investors Keep Buying
news.Content: The JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (NYSEMKT: JEPI) has become a $44 billion giant and the largest in the covered call ETF category. The fund is a relatively easy sell for income seekers too -- a low-volatility, large-cap portfolio with an 8% yield that's paid monthly. It seems to keep drawing in money regardless of its performance. It became a Wall Street darling in 2022, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI) has become a large player in the covered call ETF category, attracting assets with its high yield and low-volatility strategy.
• JEPI's performance has lagged the S&P 500 since the beginning of 2023, returning 34% compared to the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF's 99%.
• The ETF generates income primarily through selling call options on the S&P 500, meaning its income levels are driven by volatility rather than corporate performance.
• JEPI is suitable for conservative income-seeking investors but is not designed to outperform the market in a bullish rally, potentially lagging the S&P 500 more often than not.
news.Keywords: Covered Call ETF, Income Strategy, Volatility, Underperformance, Capital Growth

news.Title: Prediction: This Will Be the Next $1 Trillion Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Stock, According to Jensen Huang
news.Content: In the race to build ever-larger artificial intelligence (AI) systems, connectivity is quietly emerging as the next critical bottleneck after raw compute and the need for high-bandwidth memory. At the recent Computex conference, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang underscored this shift by publicly endorsing Marvell Technology (NASDAQ: MRVL) -- calling the chipmaker the next trillion-dollar company . Marvell's specialized semiconductors focus on high-speed networking, optical interconnects, and custom silicon. As hyperscalers accelerate data center buildouts, Marvell sits ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang has identified Marvell Technology (MRVL) as a potential next trillion-dollar AI chip company, highlighting connectivity as the next critical bottleneck.
• Marvell specializes in high-speed networking, optical interconnects, and custom silicon, positioning it to benefit from hyperscalers' AI data center buildouts.
• Marvell's solutions provide the high-speed fabrics, optics, and networking necessary for large-scale AI training clusters and inference deployments.
• Wall Street projects Marvell's earnings to double over the next two years, driven by AI-related networking and custom silicon adoption.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, connectivity, high-speed networking, custom silicon, trillion-dollar potential

news.Title: Coinbase's D'Agostino reveals the plan to put all of finance on one app
news.Content: Amazon started by selling books. Then it sold everything, and along the way it rebuilt the infrastructure that the rest of retail now runs on. That is the lens James Heckman, CEO of Roundtable, put to Coinbase in a conversation on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, a company that started by letting people buy and sell Bitcoin and Ethereum, proved it could be trusted with custody, and is now expanding into nearly every corner of finance. "You kind of feel like you're the Amazon of banking right now. And I'd love for you to react to that ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: What a Nightmare! Social Security's Financial Outlook Is Deteriorating Faster Than Expected.
news.Content: For most retirees, Social Security provides more than just a monthly check. Although the average monthly retired-worker benefit of $2,081.16, as of April 2026, is relatively modest, up to 90% of retirees rely on their Social Security income , in some capacity, to make ends meet. However, this guaranteed benefit for eligible retirees might not be as secure as it seems. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Social Security's financial outlook is deteriorating faster than expected, with the projected depletion of asset reserves for the Old-Age and Survivors Insurance (OASI) trust fund by Q4 2032.
• This depletion could lead to benefit cuts of 22% by Q4 2032, worsening to a 38% reduction by 2100 if the existing payout schedule is unsustainable.
• The long-term unfunded obligation has increased to an estimated $29.3 trillion from 2026 through 2100.
• While Social Security is not in danger of bankruptcy, the sustainability of current benefit levels, including cost-of-living adjustments, is at risk due to demographic shifts and past tax policies.
news.Keywords: Social Security, OASI trust fund, benefit cuts, unfunded obligation, demographic shifts

news.Title: Given the News Out of WWDC, Was Warren Buffett Right to Sell Apple Stock?
news.Content: Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) has long been the biggest name in the Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA) (NYSE: BRKB) portfolio. Berkshire's now-retired leader, Warren Buffett , often spoke glowingly about Apple and its ecosystem, calling it an "extraordinary consumer franchise" with massive brand loyalty. In 2020, he went even further, calling Apple "probably the best business I know in the world." But Buffett spent the last ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway significantly reduced its stake in Apple (AAPL) from mid-2023 to the present, despite Apple's stock price rising 50% during that period.
• Buffett acknowledged selling Apple stock 'too soon' but maintained that the reduction was necessary for portfolio diversification, as Apple had become an oversized holding.
• Apple's recent Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC) was considered underwhelming by investors, with the introduction of Siri AI failing to impress and not being available in all regions.
• The reduction in Apple holdings allowed Berkshire Hathaway to make other significant investments, including a large position in Alphabet (GOOGL), emphasizing the importance of portfolio diversification.
news.Keywords: Portfolio Management, Diversification, Consumer Electronics, Software Ecosystem, Investment Strategy

news.Title: 戰火還是升息？搞懂台股大跌真正原因　小心半路被洗下車
news.Content: 近期全球股市與台股的劇烈震盪，一旦下跌市場都在尋找下跌的理由，一下擔憂中東戰火，一下害怕通膨復燃，接著又聚焦超級央行週的動向。然而，資深分析師 蔡明翰 指出，這波拉回並非基本面轉壞，多頭格局並未改變，投資人切勿因恐慌而半路 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 近期台股劇烈震盪，分析師蔡明翰認為主因是「漲太多」，並非基本面轉壞，多頭格局未變。
• 市場擔憂中東戰火、通膨及央行升息，但蔡明翰指出，通膨升溫到升息循環通常有緩衝期，聯準會未明確轉向前，多頭趨勢不變。
• 日本央行升息對股市實質衝擊有限，過去經驗顯示，市場恐慌多源於其他因素，而非單純的日銀升息。
• 台股基本面依然堅挺，5月營收年增39.4%，顯示AI已轉化為實質營收，長線主軸仍是AI。
• 建議投資人關注AI設備、廠務工程及矽晶圓產業，並採取「不追高、遇重挫再加碼」的策略，耐心等待佈局時機。
news.Keywords: 多頭格局,漲多修正,通膨壓力,AI營收,逢低加碼

news.Content: Apple has spent years trying to convince investors that its next major growth story will not depend only on a new iPhone. news.Title: Better Stock to Buy Now: Amazon vs. Microsoft
news.Content: Two of the largest and most important companies in the world are Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) and Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) . Both companies rank among the top five largest companies in the world, coming in at fourth and fifth, respectively. However, investors may be torn between which is the better choice. On the surface, they look like completely different businesses, but the more you dig, the more you'll find they have in common. But ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft Azure and Amazon Web Services (AWS) are key cloud computing divisions for their respective companies.
• AWS accounted for 59% of Amazon's operating profits in Q1, despite generating only 21% of total revenue.

news.Title: Social Security Is Scheduled to Run Out of Money Sooner Than Previously Expected, and President Trump Is Partly to Blame

news.AI_Summary:
• The Social Security program's trust funds are now estimated to be depleted in six years, sooner than the previously expected 2033.

• Without government intervention, beneficiaries could face a 22% cut in their checks after the trust funds are depleted.

news.Title: Seniors Just Got a Big Clue on Social Security's 2027 COLA
news.Content: One of the most important aspects of Social Security is the program's annual cost-of-living adjustments, or COLAs. There are many seniors who collect Social Security for decades. If benefits weren't eligible for an inflation adjustments, retirees would be pretty much guaranteed to lose out on buying power over time. Instead, Social Security benefits are eligible for a raise each year in line with inflation. When there's an increase in the Consumer Price ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The article discusses Social Security's Cost-of-Living Adjustments (COLAs) and projects a potential 3.8% COLA for 2027.
• It explains that COLAs are tied to inflation, meaning higher COLAs often come with increased prices for consumers.
• The accuracy of the CPI-W in reflecting seniors' expenses, particularly healthcare costs, is questioned.
• The official COLA announcement is expected in October, with current projections suggesting modest relief for retirees.
news.Keywords: Social Security, COLA, inflation, retirees, CPI-W

news.Title: Americans Are Looking at a 40% Social Security Tax Hike Unless This Happens

news.AI_Summary:

• A 4.9-percentage-point increase represents a 40% tax hike, highlighting the urgency for legislative action to stabilize Social Security's finances.

news.Title: The Danger of Diversifying Without Really Diversifying
news.Content: Like many investors, I'm a huge fan of exchange-traded funds (ETFs), particularly because they combine the best features of stocks and mutual funds. When I invest in a particular company, I own only its stock. When I invest in an ETF, I own a diversified portfolio of stocks from many companies. Buying an ETF ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Investing in multiple ETFs can create an illusion of diversification if they hold overlapping stocks, leading to concentrated risk and unnecessary costs.
• Many popular ETFs, especially those tracking broad indexes or specific sectors like technology, often hold the same top companies (e.g., Nvidia, Apple, Microsoft).
• Overlapping holdings mean that market and category risks are not effectively spread across different investments.
• To ensure true diversification, investors should examine the top holdings of each ETF and utilize tools to identify and avoid significant overlap.
news.Keywords: ETF, diversification, portfolio risk, overlapping holdings, investment strategy

news.Title: Closed Rite Aid stores get surprising retail replacement
news.Content: When Rite Aid, a major pharmacy retailer, filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy a second time in 2025, it led to the shuttering of its 1,250 locations. And for landlords, it meant a large retail space wasted in a location that was already built around daily customer ...
news.AI_Summary:

• This move by Whole Foods highlights a trend of grocery chains taking advantage of prime real estate left behind by Rite Aid's collapse.

news.Title: This Social Security Move Is the Equivalent of Adding $144,000 to Your 401(k)

news.Title: Inflation Just Did Something It Hasn't Done Since 2023, and It Could Trigger a Big Move in Interest Rates (and the Stock Market)
news.Content: Inflation is a crucial economic indicator, and understanding it can help investors make more-informed decisions. It's primarily measured by the Consumer Price Index (CPI), which tracks the change in price of a basket of goods and services on a year-over-year basis. The Federal Reserve will adjust the federal funds rate (the overnight interest rate) when the CPI deviates too far from its annualized target of 2%. The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) just released its inflation ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The May Consumer Price Index (CPI) surged to an annualized rate of 4.2%, the highest since April 2023, indicating a significant uptick in inflation.
• This inflation increase, coupled with rising oil prices (WTI crude at $90, up 56% YTD) due to the U.S.-Iran conflict, is putting pressure on consumer and business costs.
• The Producer Price Index (PPI) also rose, with the energy component up 36.6%, suggesting businesses may pass costs onto consumers.
• Wall Street is now forecasting at least one interest rate hike by the end of the year, with a 66% chance according to CME Group's FedWatch tool.
• Rising interest rates are detrimental to the stock market, increasing yields on risk-free assets, raising debt repayment costs for consumers, limiting business borrowing, and potentially reducing corporate profits.
news.Keywords: inflation, CPI, interest rates, Federal Reserve, oil prices, stock market, economic indicators

news.Title: Bitcoin Perpetual Futures Are Now Available for Trading. Here's What You Need to Know.
news.Content: For years, crypto's most-used financial derivative was one that American investors technically couldn't access while complying with the law. That changed on May 29, when the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC), the federal derivatives regulator, cleared the first onshore Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) perpetual futures contract. Now, all sorts of different platforms are going to ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The CFTC has cleared the first onshore Bitcoin perpetual futures contract, making them accessible to US investors.
• Perpetual futures allow indefinite trading with leverage, but carry high liquidation risks, especially for retail investors.
• Leverage up to 100x can lead to liquidation from small price movements; over 254,000 positions were wiped out recently.
• Platforms like Hyperliquid offer a way to gain exposure by collecting fees on trading volume, with 70% of fees used for token buybacks.
• Trading perpetual futures is considered a high-risk play, suitable only for diversified crypto portfolios and experienced investors.
news.Keywords: derivatives, Bitcoin, perpetual futures, leverage, liquidation risk

As AI models become larger and more data-intensive, demand for storage solutions is rising across cloud and enterprise data centers. This trend is favoring Micron Technology’s NAND business. In the second quarter of fiscal 2026, the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) is well-positioned to benefit from the AI storage boom, with NAND revenues jumping 169% YoY to $5 billion in Q2 FY26.
• Demand for storage solutions is rising due to larger and more data-intensive AI models, favoring Micron's NAND business.
• Micron's data center NAND portfolio revenues more than doubled sequentially, driven by strong SSD demand.
• The company is introducing advanced storage solutions like G9 NAND-based PCIe Gen6 SSDs for AI environments.
• Industry NAND capacity remains disciplined, supporting healthier pricing and profitability for Micron.
news.Keywords: AI storage, NAND demand, memory market, data center SSDs, revenue growth

news.Title: Nvidia Opens Another AI Chip Door in China
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Nvidia ( NASDAQ:NVDA ) is pitching its new Vera CPUs to Chinese customers, opening ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia is offering its new Vera CPUs to Chinese customers as an alternative to its restricted GPU sales.
• Orders for Vera CPUs could begin in August, with a major Chinese cloud provider considering an initial order of over 300 servers.
• The chips will likely undergo testing in overseas data centers before wider deployment.
• Nvidia anticipates $20 billion in Vera revenue by the end of its fiscal year, but adoption hinges on software compatibility and workload migration.
• This strategy allows Nvidia to serve Chinese demand while navigating U.S. export controls.
news.Keywords: China market, AI processors, export controls, revenue diversification, product strategy

news.Title: Intel Rises as Capital Raise Talk Builds
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Intel ( INTC , Financials ) shares moved higher ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Intel (INTC) shares rose amid speculation that the company should raise fresh capital to fund its turnaround.
• The proposed capital raise would support expansion of its foundry business and capture demand for AI-related CPUs.
• A significant capital injection could strengthen Intel's balance sheet and fund growth projects without excessive debt.
• Potential dilution from issuing stock is a consideration, but it could enable necessary investments.

news.Keywords: capital raise, foundry business, AI workloads, turnaround strategy, balance sheet strengthening

news.Title: Meta Unwinds $2B Manus Deal
news.Content: This article first appeared on GuruFocus . Meta Platforms ( META , Financials ) has reportedly started unwinding ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Meta Platforms is reportedly unwinding its $2 billion acquisition of Manus due to Chinese regulatory orders.
• The company has separated operations, stopped employees from using Manus tools, and blocked Manus staff access to Meta's internal data.
• This situation highlights the increasing difficulty of cross-border AI deals amidst US-China competition for technology and talent.
• The unwind complicates Meta's AI strategy, which involves significant spending on AI tools, infrastructure, and talent.
news.Keywords: geopolitics, AI acquisition, regulatory hurdles, cross-border deal, strategic complication

news.Title: The Dip Is Here for O'Reilly Automotive. Here's Whether to Buy It or Walk Away.
news.Content: O'Reilly Automotive (NASDAQ: ORLY) doesn't usually go on losing streaks. Shares of the aftermarket auto parts retailer are currently trading 16% below their record (as of June 10). That all-time high was achieved in September 2025. This retail stock has historically been a dependable winner. It's up ...
news.AI_Summary:
• O'Reilly Automotive (ORLY) shares are trading 16% below their record high, presenting a potential buying opportunity.
• The company reported strong Q1 2026 results with same-store sales increasing 8.1% year-over-year and net income jumping 12.2%.
• O'Reilly has a consistent history of same-store sales growth for 33 consecutive years and is a strong free cash flow generator.
• The company plans to open 225-235 net new stores this year, benefiting from the increasing average age of cars on the road.
• The stock trades at a premium valuation (P/E of 29.6), but its consistent financial metric growth justifies the market's premium.
news.Keywords: aftermarket auto parts, same-store sales growth, free cash flow, stock buybacks, valuation premium

news.Title: SpaceX's Thesis Sounds Far-Fetched. Is There Any Way This Company Could Ever Make Money?
news.Content: There's an old adage that if you're a hammer, everything looks like a nail. Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue » Well, maybe when you're a rocket company, everything looks like an opportunity to launch things into space. That seems to be true for SpaceX (NASDAQ: SPCX) , which has scheduled ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SpaceX's IPO valuation of $1.75 trillion is based on capturing a $26.5 trillion AI market, which seems far-fetched.
• The company's core AI strategy involves moving power-intensive AI workloads into orbit to utilize constant solar energy.
• This strategy relies on expensive, unproven space-oriented technology and potentially faulty logic regarding profitability.
• The primary purpose of a corporation is to make money for shareholders, and extraterrestrial colonization may not yield a positive ROI.
news.Keywords: IPO valuation, AI market, space-based computing, solar energy, return on investment

news.Title: Ark Sees $300 Billion SpaceX Revenue Opportunity by Late 2020s

news.AI_Summary:

• This projection is contingent on deploying tens of gigawatts of orbital data center capacity at current rental rates.
• Starlink is identified as a near-term driver with potential for a six-month cash-on-cash return.
• Moving from Falcon 9 to Starship could generate nearly $200 billion in revenue from Starlink satellites alone within two years.
• Ark forecasts a $2.5 trillion enterprise value for SpaceX by 2030, despite a $4.28 billion Q1 loss, and suggests a potential merger with Tesla.
news.Keywords: revenue opportunity, orbital data centers, Starlink, Starship, enterprise value projection

news.Title: Everyone's Buying NVIDIA - Here Are 2 Smarter AI Stocks for 2026
news.Content: In the Artificial Intelligence (AI) space, NVIDIA Corporation NVDA has been one of the leaders during the AI boom. However, there are many other companies in the AI ecosystem that are performing extremely well as they are leaders in growing the ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron Technology (MU) and Dell Technologies (DELL) are highlighted as strong AI stocks, outperforming NVIDIA in the past year.
• Micron is benefiting from AI-driven demand for its High Bandwidth Memory (HBM), used in AI accelerators and data center servers.
• Micron reported Q2 fiscal 2026 revenues of $23.86 billion and forecasts Q3 revenues of $33.5 billion, driven by AI applications.

news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, memory, HBM, AI servers, revenue growth

news.Title: Nvidia pitches Vera CPU to Chinese clients with August delivery target
news.Content: Investing.com -- NVIDIA (NASDAQ:NVDA) has begun actively marketing its new Vera CPU to Chinese cloud and data-center clients, telling them the chip could be available as soon as August and that orders can already be placed, Reuters reported Friday, citing three sources familiar with the matter. The sales push marks a strategic pivot for Nvidia as US export controls have effectively shut its advanced GPU lineup out of the Chinese ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia is actively marketing its new Vera CPU to Chinese cloud and data-center clients, with potential August delivery.
• This move is a strategic pivot due to US export controls limiting advanced GPU sales in China, where Nvidia's market share has fallen significantly.
• CPUs face fewer restrictions than GPUs, providing an alternative entry point into the Chinese market.
• Initial interest includes a major Chinese cloud company planning to order over 300 servers with two Vera CPUs each for testing.
• The Vera CPU is priced at over $20,000 per processor, with a 256-chip rack costing approximately $10 million, indicating significant revenue potential.
news.Keywords: CPU, China market, export controls, data center, revenue potential

news.Title: Down 37%, Is Nu Holdings Stock Finally a Bargain?
news.Content: If your business is not considered a winner from artificial intelligence ( AI ), then it is likely severely underperforming the market. Consider Nu Holdings (NYSE: NU) . The digital banking giant is down 28% this year and 37% from its 52-week high, while the S&P 500 index is up more than 8% in 2026. This creates a bargain-buying opportunity for Nu Holdings stock if investors plan to hold for ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nu Holdings (NU) stock is down 28% year-to-date and 37% from its 52-week high, presenting a potential bargain opportunity.
• The company is expanding globally, with Mexico as its second-largest market generating nearly $1 billion in annual revenue.
• Nu Bank's efficiency ratio hit a record low of 17.6% last quarter, leading to net income growth outpacing revenue growth.
• Total net income has increased 4,000% over three years to $3.2 billion, with projections to reach $10 billion in five years.
• A $1 billion share repurchase program is underway, and despite risks in Latin American economies and lending to low-income individuals, the long-term outlook is positive.
news.Keywords: digital banking, global expansion, efficiency ratio, net income growth, share repurchase

news.Title: AMD “emerging as a legit second source in the GPU market” says Citi
news.Content: Investing.com -- Citi has upgraded Advanced Micro Devices to Buy from Neutral, raising its price target to $575 from $460, arguing that the company's emergence as a "legit second source ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Citi upgraded Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) to 'Buy' with a price target of $575, citing its emergence as a significant GPU market player.
• Investors currently view AMD primarily as a CPU stock, but Citi believes AMD is poised to win a substantial share of Meta's GPU business.
• Custom MI450 chips for Meta are expected to offer a lower total cost of ownership compared to merchant GPU products.
• Citi forecasts AMD's AI sales to reach $33 billion in 2027 and $50.8 billion in 2028, with a six-gigawatt deal with Meta contributing significantly.

news.Keywords: GPU market, data center, AI sales, upgrade, total cost of ownership

news.Title: These 3 Semiconductor Stocks Just Got a Bullish Nod From Citi. Buy the Dip.
news.Content: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index ($SOX) hit an all-time high of 13,998.14 on June 3, then quickly dropped 9.58% in one session, its sharpest fall in a while. The selloff followed Broadcom's (AVGO) Q2 earnings report. Even though revenue jumped 48% year-over-year (YoY) to $22.2 billion, investors were not impressed. The market wanted stronger AI guidance and more big customer wins. Because Broadcom is seen as a key signal for the chip sector, its cautious tone dragged the rest of the space lower, including Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Micron Technology (MU), Arm Holdings (ARM), and Marvell Technology (MRVL). Even ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Citi views the recent pullback in semiconductor stocks as a healthy reset, not the start of a deeper decline.
• The firm identifies Broadcom (AVGO), Texas Instruments (TXN), and Applied Materials (AMAT) as attractive investment opportunities.
• These companies represent different segments of the semiconductor supply chain, including AI chips, analog components, and manufacturing equipment.
• Broadcom's Q2 fiscal 2026 revenue increased 48% YoY to $22.19 billion, and it expects Q3 revenue to jump 84%.
• Texas Instruments focuses on analog and embedded chips, experiencing strong stock performance with a 45% increase over the past 52 weeks.
news.Keywords: semiconductor sector, stock reset, AI infrastructure, analog chips, manufacturing equipment

news.Title: Exclusive-South Korea's SK Hynix to opt for Nasdaq for planned US listing, sources say
news.Content: By Heekyong Yang and Yantoultra Ngui SEOUL/SINGAPORE, June 12 (Reuters) - South Korean memory chipmaker SK Hynix is looking to choose the Nasdaq for its planned U.S. listing, two sources familiar with the matter ‌said, opting for the technology-heavy bourse to capitalize on investor appetite for AI-linked stocks. More from ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SK Hynix, a major memory chipmaker and key supplier to Nvidia, is planning a U.S. listing on the Nasdaq as early as August.
• The company's stock has surged 230% this year, reaching a market value over $1 trillion, driven by strong demand for AI-linked stocks.
• Nasdaq was chosen over NYSE to capitalize on investor appetite for technology and growth companies, potentially leading to higher valuations.
• The planned listing aims to broaden SK Hynix's investor base and profile among global investors, with potential to raise up to $14 billion.
news.Keywords: IPO, Nasdaq listing, AI demand, memory chips, investor base

news.Title: Micron and Sandisk Are Up Big During the Past Year. Can the Run Continue?
news.Content: If you had the foresight to invest in Micron (NASDAQ: MU) and Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) stock during this time last year, you look like a genius right now. Micron is up about 700%, while Sandisk is up more than 3,800%. Those are monster returns in a short time frame, and would thrill any investor. But that doesn't matter anymore. What really matters ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Micron (MU) and Sandisk (SNDK) have experienced significant stock price appreciation over the past year, with Micron up ~700% and Sandisk up over 3,800%.
• Both companies are benefiting from a memory chip shortage driven by the AI build-out, leading to high demand from hyperscalers.
• Analysts project strong revenue growth for Micron (63% in FY27) and Sandisk (122% in FY27), driven by sustained demand and limited production capacity.
• Despite recent gains, valuations are considered reasonable with forward P/E ratios around 9x, suggesting potential for further upside, though supply/demand dynamics require monitoring.
news.Keywords: Semiconductor shortage, AI infrastructure, memory chips, revenue growth, valuation

news.Title: Bank of America resets AMD stock price target
news.Content: Advanced Micro Devices ( AMD ) has gained about 128.45% year to date, at the time of writing, Thursday afternoon, June 11. Meanwhile, the SPDR S&P 500 index ( SPY ) is up about 8.47% in the same period. The company has outpaced the S&P 500 by a huge margin. That is impressive, but what is driving these gains? AMD is a semiconductor company that makes CPUs and GPUs, and its stock has rallied amid the AI boom. Key news items for ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Keywords: Server CPUs, TAM expansion, market share, AI accelerators, price target

news.Title: Hedge funds sold broader tech ahead of SpaceX IPO, JPMorgan data shows

news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: S&P Futures Climb as U.S.-Iran Peace Deal Nears, SpaceX Debut in Focus
news.Content: June S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESM26) are up +0.46% this morning, extending yesterday’s gains as fresh signs emerged that the U.S. and Iran are moving closer to a peace deal. Iran’s semi-official Mehr news agency reported that a draft agreement consists of 14 provisions. These include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz within 30 days, the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets, and 60 days of negotiations over nuclear issues. Iran’s foreign ministry told state-run media that a framework text was close to being finalized. Bloomberg reported on Friday that the agreement could be signed as early ...
news.AI_Summary:
• S&P 500 futures are up, influenced by optimism surrounding a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal and the upcoming SpaceX debut.
• Chip and AI infrastructure stocks rallied, with SanDisk (SNDK) jumping over 14% and Lam Research (LRCX) surging more than 12%.
• Economic data showed mixed signals with higher-than-expected PPI but weaker core PPI, while jobless claims rose unexpectedly.
• Market sentiment is focused on geopolitical developments, the SpaceX IPO, and upcoming consumer sentiment data, with a high probability of no Fed rate change in June.
news.Keywords: Geopolitical risk, AI stocks, economic indicators, market sentiment, SpaceX IPO

news.Title: IonQ, Rigetti, and D-Wave Are Surging Again. Is Quantum Computing Finally Real?
news.Content: After a rough stretch, quantum computing stocks are hot once again. Though they've retreated somewhat in the last week, shares of IonQ (NYSE: IONQ) , Rigetti Computing (NASDAQ: RGTI) , and D-Wave Quantum (NYSE: QBTS) have gained 50% or more from the end of March. So, what's going on? And is now the time to buy? Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Quantum computing stocks, including IonQ, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum, have seen significant gains (50%+) since late March.
• The sector is experiencing renewed interest, partly due to a $2 billion federal funding allocation under the CHIPS and Science Act.
• Despite technological advancements and government investment, widespread commercial viability for quantum computing is still estimated to be a decade or more away.
• These stocks are highly speculative, trading at extreme valuations with little revenue or profit, making them sensitive to market sentiment and broader market corrections.

news.Title: How Musk's tactics left investors clamoring for SpaceX stock and ignoring risks
news.Content: By Echo Wang NEW YORK, June 12 (Reuters) - In recent years, Elon Musk has been picky about whom he allowed to buy in to SpaceX, the rocket ship company making its highly anticipated stock market debut this week. One investor said that when SpaceX was privately held, he used a connection with a cousin of the billionaire entrepreneur to buy $10 million worth of stock in the satellite maker in 2018. A portfolio manager at a $500 billion U.S. fund said he had to use his relationship with a board member of the Musk-led Tesla for an ‌allocation of ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Elon Musk's tactics have created high demand for SpaceX stock among investors, despite significant risks associated with the company's operations and governance.
• Pre-IPO investors, including those with connections to Musk or Tesla, underwent rigorous screening and interviews, often with limited financial information, to secure allocations.
• SpaceX is expected to list with a valuation of $1.75 trillion, a massive increase from its 2018 valuation of $30 billion.
• The IPO includes 30% for individual investors, raising concerns about potential risks such as weak corporate governance, loss-making operations, and ambitious, hard-to-value goals like colonizing Mars.
news.Keywords: Pre-IPO investment, valuation surge, corporate governance, speculative risks, investor access

news.Content: Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) and Intel (NASDAQ: INTC) are popular with investors right now because the artificial intelligence (AI) boom represents a significant growth opportunity for both companies. news.AI_Summary:

• Morningstar suggests a 44% downside for Micron (target $500 from $898), citing a lack of durable competitive advantage and commodity-like products.
• J.P. Morgan indicates a 60% downside for Intel (target $45 from $115), highlighting market share losses and manufacturing challenges.

news.Content: Investing.com -- SpaceX begins trading on Nasdaq today under the ticker SPCX, targeting a valuation of approximately $1.75 trillion and seeking to raise $75 billion, more than 2.5 times the previous record set by Saudi Aramco (TADAWUL:2222) in 2019.
